chunks.text:
Row Level Security (RLS) lets you restrict which rows a given user can read or modify. You enable RLS on a table by running ALTER TABLE your_table ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY. Once enabled, all queries are filtered through policies you define. Policies use auth.uid() to reference the currently authenticated user. A policy like USING (user_id = auth.uid()) ensures each user sees only their own rows. RLS policies apply to SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, and DELETE independently.

Realtime Channels provide pub-sub built on PostgreSQL logical replication. When you subscribe to a channel, the client receives INSERT, UPDATE, and DELETE events as they happen. Channels are scoped by table name or custom topic string. The client library exposes channel.on('postgres_changes', callback) for database events and channel.on('broadcast', callback) for arbitrary payloads sent between clients.

Auth with magic links removes the need for passwords entirely. You call supabase.auth.signInWithOtp({ email }) and the user receives a one-time link. Clicking the link completes sign-in and returns a valid session with access and refresh tokens. The token pair is automatically managed by the client library, which handles refresh before expiry. You can combine magic link auth with RLS policies that reference auth.uid() to build a fully serverless, passwordless application.

Edge Functions let you deploy server-side TypeScript on Deno at the edge. Each function exports a default handler receiving a Request and returning a Response. You deploy with supabase functions deploy my-function. Edge Functions access your database through the service role key, bypassing RLS for admin operations. You set secrets using supabase secrets set MY_SECRET=value, available as Deno.env.get('MY_SECRET') at runtime. Cold starts are typically under 200 milliseconds. Functions can invoke other functions or external APIs, making them suitable for webhook handlers, scheduled tasks, and custom API endpoints that need server-side logic beyond what RLS and PostgREST provide out of the box with their automatic schema-based routing and policy enforcement mechanisms.

Storage signed URLs give temporary access to files in private buckets. You call supabase.storage.from('bucket').createSignedUrl('path/to/file', expiresIn) where expiresIn is seconds until expiry. The URL contains a cryptographic token granting read access without authentication. You can also create signed upload URLs for unauthenticated single-file uploads.

e expiresIn is seconds until expiry. The URL contains a cryptographic token granting read access without authentication. You can also create signed upload URLs for unauthenticated single-file uploads.

PostgREST auto-generates a RESTful API from your PostgreSQL schema. Every table, view, and function becomes an endpoint. Filtering uses query parameters like ?column=eq.value. The API supports embedding related tables via select=*,related_table(*). PostgREST respects RLS policies, so the API is automatically secured per-user.
